symbols.prefix: B(s,z)=1/2 a c^{-alpha} int_c^{c exp(s)} y^{alpha-1} exp(-y) dy

mellinTotalStatus.label: Xi atom Mellin integral
mellinTotalStatus.reason: With y=c exp(v), dv=dy/y and exp(beta v)exp(i tau z v) =(y/c)^(beta+i tau z).  This gives X(z)=1/2 a c^{-alpha} Gamma(alpha,c).

movingBoundarySplitStatus.label: moving boundary split
movingBoundarySplitStatus.reason: Splitting the y-integral at c exp(s) gives the incomplete-gamma prefix B(s,z) plus the tail integral from c exp(s) to infinity.

diagonalVolterraTailStatus.label: diagonal Volterra tail transport
diagonalVolterraTailStatus.reason: In the tail use v=s+u.  The factor exp(beta(s+u)-c exp(s+u)) separates into the base value at s times exp(beta u-c exp(s)(exp(u)-1)), exactly the Volterra ratio atom.

boundaryConcomitantStatus.label: Mellin boundary concomitant
boundaryConcomitantStatus.reason: Differentiating the prefix endpoint c exp(s) gives B'(s,z)=1/2 a exp(beta s-c exp(s))exp(i tau z s), and B(0,z)=0.

primitiveMuTraceStatus.label: primitive Mu trace
primitiveMuTraceStatus.reason: For F(s)=int_s^L f(t)dt, integration by parts gives int_0^L B(s,z)f(s)ds=int_0^L B'(s,z)F(s)ds because B(0,z)=0 and F(L)=0.

proof: Substitute y=c exp(v) in the half-line Mellin atom integral.
Split the resulting incomplete-gamma integral at y=c exp(s).
Use v=s+u in the tail to identify the normalized Volterra ratio atom.
Differentiate the moving upper endpoint to obtain B'(s,z).
Integrate by parts against F(s)=int_s^L f(t)dt to get the Mu trace.
scope: This theorem proves only the exact symbolic atom identities.  Trace closure, repair positivity, and positive-cone limits are handled by separate theorem objects.
